nodes.x: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.

give glory to Him, for the hour of His judgment is come — shadowing forth the restoration of all things that have been spoken by the mouths of all thine holy Prophets since the world began — establishing the pure principles of the Eternal Heavens, which constitute the laws of the kingdom of our God upon the earth ; opening up anew the great principles of revelation and communication with the Gods…
O Lord God, thou hast begun to shew thy servant thy greatness, and thy mighty hand: for what God is there in heaven or in earth, that can do according to thy works, and according to thy might?

¶ Thus saith God the Lord, he that created the heavens, and stretched them out; he that spread forth the earth, and that which cometh out of it; he that giveth breath unto the people upon it, and spirit to them that walk therein:
God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.

Therefore night shall be unto you, that ye shall not have a vision; and it shall be dark unto you, that ye shall not divine; and the sun shall go down over the prophets, and the day shall be dark over them.
Kingdom, and our soul's salvation at this Conference. “Day unto day uttereth speech,” said the ancient man of God, “and night unto night
But it shall be one day which shall be known to the Lord, not day, nor night: but it shall come to pass, that at evening time it shall be light.

Therefore, there shall be one day and a night and a day, as if it were one day and there were no night; and this shall be unto you for a sign; for ye shall know of the rising of the sun and also of its setting; therefore they shall know of a surety that there shall be two days and a night; nevertheless the night shall not be darkened; and it shall be the night before he is born.
The light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not; nevertheless, the day shall come when you shall comprehend even God, being quickened in him and by him.
There is no speech nor language, where their voice is not heard.
They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;
They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths.
They have mouths, but they speak not: eyes have they, but they see not:
To the law and to the testimony: if they speak not according to this word, it is because there is no light in them.
They have hands, but they handle not: feet have they, but they walk not: neither speak they through their throat.
Their line is gone out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. In them hath he set a tabernacle for the sun,
And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,
And they answered the angel of the Lord that stood among the myrtle trees, and said, We have walked to and fro through the earth, and, behold, all the earth sitteth still, and is at rest.
And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end, and none shall help him.
And they shall keep thy charge, and the charge of all the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary and the altar, that neither they, nor ye also, die.
Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, and rejoiceth as a strong man to run a race.
But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
His going forth is from the end of the heaven, and his circuit unto the ends of it: and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof.
He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.
Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
The way of life is above to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.
The law of the Lord is perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the Lord is sure, making wise the simple.
This is eternal lives—to know the only wise and true God, and Jesus Christ, whom he hath sent. I am he. Receive ye, therefore, my law.
For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he will save us.
How do ye say, We are wise, and the law of the Lord is with us? Lo, certainly in vain made he it; the pen of the scribes is in vain.

The laws that the Lord has given are not fully perfect, because the
people could not receive them in their perfect fulness; but they can
receive a little here and a little there, a little today and a little
tomorrow, a little more next week, and a little more in advance of
that next year, if they make a wise improvement upon every little they
receive; if they do not, they are left in the shade,…
The statutes of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes.
And ye shall keep my statutes, and do them: I am the Lord which sanctify you.
And this commandment shall be given unto the elders of my church, that every man which will embrace it with singleness of heart may be ordained and sent forth, even as I have spoken.
Let your heart therefore be perfect with the Lord our God, to walk in his statutes, and to keep his commandments, as at this day.
Ye shall diligently keep the commandments of the Lord your God, and his testimonies, and his statutes, which he hath commanded thee.
The fear of the Lord is clean, enduring for ever: the judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
The fear of the Lord tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.
Wherefore now let the fear of the Lord be upon you; take heed and do it: for there is no iniquity with the Lord our God, nor respect of persons, nor taking of gifts.
The fear of the Lord is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.

“Fear,” said he, “the Lord, and keep His 
commandments.’ For if you keep the com- 
mandments of God, you will be powerful in 
every action, and every one of your actions will 
be incomparable. For, fearing the Lord, you 
will do all things well. This is the fear which 
you ought to have, that you may be saved. But 
fear not the devil; for, fearing the Lord, you 
will have dominion over the devil,…
More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold: sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.
My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.
For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.
How sweet are thy words unto my taste! yea, sweeter than honey to my mouth!
My son, eat thou honey, because it is good; and the honeycomb, which is sweet to thy taste:
The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.
Moreover by them is thy servant warned: and in keeping of them there is great reward.
Now these are thy servants and thy people, whom thou hast redeemed by thy great power, and by thy strong hand.
Let them curse, but bless thou: when they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.
For thy word’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all these great things, to make thy servant know them.
Who can understand his errors? cleanse thou me from secret faults.
Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous sins; let them not have dominion over me: then shall I be upright, and I shall be innocent from the great transgression.
Deal with thy servant according unto thy mercy, and teach me thy statutes.

And all this that the innocent among you may not be condemned with the unjust; and that the guilty among you may not escape; because I, the Lord, have promised unto you a crown of glory at my right hand.
Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
And enter not into judgment with thy servant: for in thy sight shall no man living be justified.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, my servants, that inasmuch as you have forgiven one another your trespasses, even so I, the Lord, forgive you.
Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight, O Lord, my strength, and my redeemer.
Give ear to my words, O Lord, consider my meditation.

1. Listen to the voice of Jesus Christ, your Lord, your God, and
    your Redeemer, whose word is quick and powerful.
My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.
